review_text: Great location close to everything

Kamers gedateerd
Goede service bij laat inchecken
Vlotte verbinding met Adelaide stad, zowel met auto, bus, als trein
Verschillende eetgelegenheden in de buurt
Very old smelly rooms.. lovely location though
Great place to relax
Very good location with very nice amenities.

We recently stayed at Discovery Parks – Adelaide Beachfront (Semaphore) and unfortunately our cabin was in a very poor state on arrival.

There was mould in the shower, hair on the bathroom floor, crumbs and hair in cupboards, and rubbish including clothing left under the beds and months worth of dust. The floors were so dirty our feet turned black from walking on them.

Due to the condition, we couldn’t allow our children to crawl or be on the floor, which made the stay quite stressful and not at all relaxing.

We raised this with management after our stay and, despite providing details and evidence, were advised the issues were not present at check-in. (Basically telling me I am lying) A partial refund was offered, which we did not feel reflected the condition of the accommodation.

Disappointing experience overall.
Do not recommend.
Great location next to beach. Rooms are nice but single pull out was very uncomfortable. Public transport was close and park is clean
Good location, vibrant and safe.

We were excited to stay in the Superior Cabin with our family of 5. Unfortunately the room was just filthy. There was clumps of hair in the sink from people before us. The walls had gunk and hand prints on them and the smell of mould was obvious. The window sills were absolutely caked with dust, and don’t even get me started on what was under the mattresses of the bunk beds - dead bugs and filth. Nothing appeared to be clean. Stains on the mattress protectors and all over the blankets. The fan for the shower did not actually do anything so it was a struggle to breathe with the steam and the shower door was broken and didn’t shut all the way so the floor was soaking. Everything was very old and run down and honestly it just felt so dirty that we wanted to spend as little time as possible actually in the cabin.
Excellent  place for a weekend  away
Drove 9 hours for a disgusting cabin, every sheet had stains on the bed, the wifi didn''t work and at 2am in the morning, kids are running around screaming bloody murder.
We are on 12 months Journey and found this park to be in the top 5 we have stay at.

Had a wonderful stay at this Discovery Park with my partner and our 2  puppers,  with access onto the beautiful beach.  Michael the manager looked after us extremely well and was able to secure us an extra night in the same cabin after doing some juggling around.  We were vey appreciative and grateful to him, we would thoroughly recommend staying here, our cabin was clean, well maintained and very comfortable.  If ever over in Adelaide again, will definitely be back.
Great place to stay. Close to ocean and shopping near by

Stayed here for a week over the LIV golf event and was pretty disappointed in our stay
Cramped into the sites , no pressure or hot water in the showers and ladies on front desk had an attitude the whole time
Ended up getting moved to an ensuite site on our last night as this was the only site available which are even more cramped in and our neighbours had 4 dogs that didn’t stop whining
Overall experience wasn’t great and we won’t be back

Fantastic stay at this park. The pet friendly cabins are amazing, the best we have ever stayed in. Having stayed in both of the pet friendly cabins now can 100% recommend to anyone thinking of staying here and they want to bring their furbaby with them. Staff were so friendly and it is clear the guests comfort has been thought about in every way. Thank you we will definitely be back!
1 of my favourite parks to walk out the gate to that beautiful beach and everytime I stay the staff have been beautiful!
Had a powered site expensive at $400+for the week They pack you in like sardines,was good to free camp again.

We loved it here. Right on the beach, just a few minutes from Semaphore main street where there is everything you could want. If promenading on the esplanade is your thing then this is it.  Beautiful parks, gorgeous sandy beaches, facilities every 100 metres, kiosks, playgrounds, shady park benches and a jetty. For us the best feature was the bikeway from West Lakes Shore all the way to Outer Harbour.
Very easy check in, stayed in a cabin, rooms very clean and nice, beds pretty comfy all round good place to stay great location would definitely stay again
Great location and fantastic facilities
Thankyou for the memories
Good place to stay for a short time, good clean amenities
Fantastic place to stay with kids. Cleanest communal bathrooms ive seen in a park. Only down was the shower door felt like it was going to break. We'll be back!
We absolutely loved staying here the kids had a ball. The cabin was a geat size for 4 adults and 2 kids. The manager not sure of his name was amazing, We will be back thankyou.

We booked a cabin for 3 nights, over new years. Obviously aware that new years would be pricey, was not expecting in excess of $400 per night for a cabin for 2 adults, 1 child, 2 babies, especially when we provided the babies sleeping spaces. The washing machine in the cabin had not been cleaned in a while, nor had the filters been emptied (ran 2 cleaning cycles to get the mouldy/musty smell out of it). Could be beneficial for a small vacuum to be provided to clean the cloors since the mop head was unuasble. That being said the free entertainment in the park was a hit with the 5yo. LOVED the daily coffee van. The beach was walking distance & the park overall was maintained well. The swimming pools are a great size & mostly under shade sails which is great.

Stayed at this park for 8 days over new years 25/26 with a large group of 5 vans on sites. Beautiful location . On the third night thieves ran through the park and stole bikes, food from the camp kitchen, shoes from sites and a generator. The park sends out lock up your valuables texts, however plays down the fact that the thieves walk through the park during the day and take stuff while you are at your sites- for example two electric bikes were attempted to be stolen in broad daylight and toiletries from the shower.

There is no security in the park at all with these problems, not even at night and the staff are uninterested in helping or showing empathy toward  the situation and just tell you to go the police.The gates to the beach rarely work and I am not sure how often they change the code.

If you search the park on the internet it describes the significant problem of high crime rate and items being stolen regularly. If you are going to run a park with full knowledge of this, invest in better security and consequences. Why wouldn't the thieves come to this park? There is nothing to stop them.

Any holidayer would be happy to pay extra to ensure security and it is disappointing for such a careless approach to be considered sufficient from a discovery park.
Had christmas  there was nice rest rooms were very clean staff were friendly  . They had santa for the kids and a barbecue  on boxing day
Very happy with the staff member that helped us. He bent over backwards to be accomodating. Will come back again.
Wow its a fantastic  park with very helpful staff the amenities are very clean we have an ensuite site the bathroom is amazing so clean a credit to discovery  parks well done

Pros: location close to beach

Cons: price per night changed between booking and paying on arrival. Staff member became defensive, dismissive and non communicative when asked about the price difference advertised online.
Refused to stay on those terms so can’t comment on quality of park.
Location is so good
Nice park for a stay in the beach, we took our van, reasonably priced for a powered ensuite site
Excellent park - friendly,  welcoming staff

Nice caravan and cabin park right next to the beach at Semaphore with good beach access. We stayed in one of their economy cabins which was comfortable and well appointed. Friendly helpful staff when we were there. Handy for Adelaide Airport. Would stay here again

Good location, cleanliness and facilities. Nearby park is a ripper!

Fun times to be had on the nearby beach.

I previously wrote a scathing review because i was extremely tired from travelling.
However I ended up staying here the next night in the villa.
The villas are awesome with heaps of room. Aircon is a bit confusing to work but it did.
Showers are great with goid pressure and volume but just a fraction small. Needs more holders for personal shampoo etc.
Beds were comfy and clean with a few fluffy bits but that's nothing.
The reception really friendly and helpful.
Great access to the beach.

I really enjoyed it A+

Overcrowded, unclean cabins
Noisy, and  drunken yobbos wandering around until 2 am
Bad experience
Camper
Close to the beach. We were on a powered site with family in a cabin which they kindly upgraded so they could be closer to our site. Great beach for long walks.

From the moment I arrived for my 4 day stay, they couldn’t be more helpful! The staff were very accommodating and nothing was too much, even helping me every day with my awning…. The park itself is beautiful, very soft manicured grass, clean sites and the beach nice and close.The pool was beautiful and clean too.The park kitchen, laundry, bathroom and showers were pristine and cleaned out every day. The bbq area was lovely and clean/hygienic every time I went to use it for my cook ups. I will certainly be back. My Campervan and I had a lovely getaway, thank you ! 5/5 stars 🌟

Place is nicely located next to the beach with a side gate access!Downside is that internet and wifi access is pretty poor. Cabin cleanliness is average as expected from campsites. There are some ants crawling around.

Excellent......up there at the top of our Gday/Discovery park stays. Amenities are spot on.
Level & generous sites, manicured grounds. Front of house staff always jovial.
Sema4 surf club adjoins with Point Malcolm recreation reserve.
Had a great time, but what ever they wash the bathroom ensuite floor with was incredibly slippery. My partner slipped and broke her toe. But still had a wonderful time
BEAHC KAW KAW
Beautiful spot and well maintained

Great location in Adelaide, beach just out back gate and bike path that runs along beach. Amenities clean, park well presented and very quiet. Only on let down is sites are good size but very tight to get into. We have stayed before and it is our perfered place to stay when in Adelaide with the van. I do recommend this park

I stay here often, and have to say it's the best caravan park I've stayed at. Always clean, well-maintained, well-designed modern units, and generous with free upgrades in off-peak season.  Fantastic place to stay.

I have stayed here twice now and disappointed with the room I just stayed in 588 dollars for the weekend and was not happy with the fact the air-conditioner was so dirty I had to clean it dishes left in the dishwasher food all in it aswell I am a super host and would never allow that

Stayed 3 days and no hot water in showers for 2 of those. No signs to alert you so you only found out then you were stripped down. Manager didn't seem to care and didn't offer compensation. We didn't get what we paid for.

I stayed this week with a last minute booking. Having a small dog, inevitably we have limited options but, the 1980's un-refurbished cabin, with three different types of vinyl on the floors, a kitchen oven which looks like it would blow up and, a bed so old it sinks in the night.||But the bigger issue is around the way they make up policies including taking photos of ID's on a personal mobile phone for guests arriving after hours. This is a company that has access to online check-in apps, secure and convenient but won't pay for them making after hours check in a nightmare.||I made my booking on the day, checked the website and understood things based on the information available. Within an hour of making the booking the first call came through, not to thank me for booking, not to welcome me, but to ask what time I was arriving (this info was already supplied). When I mentioned 5.30pm, I was told I couldn't check in at that time (despite the website showing I could check in till 7pm). I was told I had to pay a $200 deposit (not mentioned online) and, present my ID on arrival and must arrive by 5pm. ||My instinct was to cancel, I wish I had done that, at least this would have meant my data would be secure.||I called back and said I couldnt get there till 5.30pm, they agreed I could pay the 'surprise' deposit by card over the phone. No doubt my card details and CVV were written down and probably now in the SA tip.||When I arrived, I showed my ID, the staff member refused to accept it unless she took a photo, when I refused to allow this, she refused to give me the key. At no point was I advised that some random team member would be wanting to take a photo of my ID, on an insecure device before arriving... it isn't 2010 - surely G'Day would have learnt something from all the data hacks going on but no, its either there way or you sleep on the street.||This is obviously a property that has problems with junkies and crack ho's, to demand a security deposit only for same day bookings indicates they are trying to avoid issues. If this was known upfront, it wouldn't have been an issue but, my issue is about making up policies not displayed and then saying 'they are standard'. ||If a policy is standard, then it would be listed. If G'day have no concerns about the safety of guest information being recorded on private mobile phones... I would be surprised.||Won't be back - cabin was old and out of date, staff lacked empathy and the business lacks even the most basic of professional standards when it comes to data protection.
Loved it with my 2 kids.  And  it was definitely busy and staff were lovely

Had a caravan site with plenty of room. Amenities were spotless, as was the whole park. Great location right on the water with a walkway at the back door. Close to amazing restaurants and shopping. Staff is very obliging.  Great stay.

Very disappointed in the accommodation we were placed in. The cabin which was pet friendly but far from human friendly absolutely stunk of cigarettes. Air condition remote held together with electrical tape.
Floors were filthy covered in sand and the blanket in main bedroom was filthy. Cigarette butts laying on ground outside cabin. Four guest but beds were not made in bunk room which we found very poor service. There was no toaster in kitchen.
I would never recommend this park to stay and I couldn’t wait to get out of there to the next accommodation so I could have a shower and wash my clothes which by the way smelt of cigarettes as well. Shame on you Discovery Park Semaphore. We’ve always in the past had good experiences.

This caravan park is a huge disappointment and not worth the money. Our ensuite was dirty and poorly maintained, with the bin left unemptied for an entire week — completely unacceptable and unhygienic. It felt like cleanliness wasn’t a priority at all.

There’s only one dump point for the entire park, which caused unnecessary delays and frustration during our stay. The front office staff were rude and unhelpful, making what should have been a simple check-in process unnecessarily difficult.

To make matters worse, the intermittent barking of dogs throughout the day and night made it difficult to relax or get a good night’s sleep. If that’s your idea of a holiday, then this place might suit you. For everyone else, look elsewhere.

Discovery Parks should be ashamed to have this park under their name. I wouldn’t stay here again, even if it was free.

We canceled a B&B booking and decided to go with a caravan park instead and tow our home behind is.|We couldn’t  be happier with our choice as this park is fantastic and has direct beach access for those afternoon walks. Pet friendly is a bonus so we could bring along our little girl. Our ensuite site is tucked away in the back corner and is just perfect and quiet.|We would highly recommend especially for families and if we’re ever in the area in the future we will definitely stay again 👍🏼👍🏼👍🏼

Stayed there twice now, first time was cabin 28, that cabin was nice. Second time was cabin 50, that cabin is a little longer in the tooth. Tiny bit of wriggle around the bed, bed was too soft, we both rolled to the centre, the floor in the bathroom is uneven.
Great park. Close to beach. Dog friendly. Great ensuites.

We had a wonderful week stay here. Park is gorgeous,  couldn't fault anything. Bbq areas, playground,  amenities everything was fantastic.  I was in an economy cabin with my son, we had a minor issue, went told staff, and straight away, maintenance came over and fixed it. Beautiful park next door, beach next to it and even a mini train line. Great location. The only fault wasn't even about park, but with Veolia garbage truck waking me up twice in a week at 7am goin through park. 🤣. But the park. Absolutely fabulous.

stay here for work in the newer cabins.
it's perfect for a quick few nights stay. small kitchen, good shower and always clean.

2 adults in the ocean view cabin for 2 nights. Room was clean and was big enough for us. Water pressure for the shower was good and hot. Staff was friendly and helpful. Even when I left something behind they held it until I could organise postage to get it sent back to me. Only bad point my van was to tall for the car spot I wish I knew before booking that room but the staff organise a very close spot to park it.
Friendly staff,great location and great for pets.

We recently stayed on a powered caravan site for 2 nights with our kids. The site was really tiny despite providing van dimensions. We were unable to have our awning extended fully as our van and car wouldn’t fit. All of our neighbours had the same issue. The waste water drain was blocked with neighbours just letting waste water run onto grass. My husband managed to unblock it. The site was dirty, someone had obviously cut their hair on the site and it hadn’t been blower vacuumed, as I have little ones that like to crawl on lawn I found this really gross and it wasn’t a little bit of hair it was clumps. All of these issues were identified shortly after checking in so it was clear nothing had been done to prepare the site. Also people are allowed to smoke on site? I don’t know if this is normal but it was very off putting having our neighbours second hand smoke coming in our van. I checked the smoking situation with reception and they said it was fine to smoke. On a positive note the location is good, check in was fast and easy, amenities were very clean.
Great caravan park. Got all amenities. Close to the beach. Park activities for kids. Walking trails for your 4-legged pooch.
Absolutely fantastic staff, we stayed in a pet friendly room and was absolutely happy with the room
Excellent manager and staff, extremely helpful and great place to stay, repeat customer. Highly recommend

Loved this park. Great for the family. Beautiful playground and nice pool. Check in was so easy and smooth. Cabin was spacious and clean. Bathroom shower was good pressure and didn’t go too cold. My wife loved the coffee at the cafe, which was nice to have onsite and not need to leave!

Spent a night during the school holidays with my kids. Small and tidy park. Room facilities was fine, toilet tucked into the corner of the bathroom really tight and awkward spot. The bunk bed was loud every timemy kids tossed n turned at night, so kids had to sleep in my bed, which left me unrested. I spoke to one of the office girls about it in the morning and she couldn't have cared less. Won't be back, will stick to the other parks we normally frequent, this park has been one of the worst regarding your staff being friendly and inviting.
Stella.

Quiet little park ,clean facilities.
5 minutes from the beach .
Really nice 2klm stroll to Semaphore shopping and entertainment precinct.
Have a nice stay,.
Had the best week away. 3 teenage boys with us. Loved this park. Best one in Adelaide Iv stayed at. Great staff perfect location. Thankyou for a great week away. We will be back 😀😀😀😀😀😀😀😀😀😀😀😀😀😀

Nice and quiet holiday park.
It was nice to see matinance around the park.
Very clean.

We Stayed at your  cabin park this time last year .
Our grandchildren had a ball on the GO carts and we all love the place .All  Staff made us very welcome nothing was to much.
Our cabin was very close to the beach which we enjoyed and the cabin was very clean bedding and towels were also very clean.
We enjoyed it so much we have tolled everyone how good it was and that shopping was near by and takeaway across the road .
We are about to book soon to come back .
I Would highly  recommend this park . Can't say enough about it .
Beautiful accommodation, clean and close to the beach, nice to on my hometown turf on the Ninche
Cabin had great shower and air con, short stroll to beach. Had to make daughter's bed. Not quite understanding this considering overall cost. Cabin a little tired.
Cabins very clean, modern and comfortable. Beautiful location, excellently maintained facilities.

Much to love about this spot. It's right by the beach and it's clean and safe. The staff are lovely. They even directed me to a little book exchange place on the Hart Street roundabout! A quiet, peaceful place to loll about, relax, swim, and read a good book.

Stayed at Adelaide Beachfront Park for a week in February '25. Convenient location right on the beach and not far from the local shops and restaurants. The tent/caravan sites were a good size and overall the facilities were well maintained. Amenities were modern and kept clean even though the park was quite busy at the time we stayed. The only downside worth mentioning is that the road noise at the very front of the park where we were situated was quite intrusive. No doubt it would be quieter  towards the beach side. The park has on site cabins as well as ensuite sites. Kid friendly with a playpark, pool and jumping pillow. Staff were friendly and accommodating. The park is also dog friendly but check conditions prior to booking.

We stayed for three nights and enjoyed our cabin. Our experience was enhanced from a complimentary upgrade to a deluxe cabin. Check-in was quick and efficient, and the location  on the beachfront at Semaphore Park is excellent. The park is undergoing some improvements and expansion at the moment, so there can be some noise from workers in some sections of the park during the day, but the improvements will be excellent when completed. Water pressure in the cabin is very low, especially during the day. The pool area is small but very nice and there are good communal facilities for those camping. We enjoyed our brief stay and will return when we come back to zadelaide if there is availability.

see a nice deluxe cabin online and get something looking nothing like the picture jammed in the back corner of the park.
totally misleading and not what we paid for.

Amazing location so close to the beach but best of all is the incredible customer service the ladies at Adelaide Beachfront went above & beyond for me in fixing a booking error through Bookings.com. We will be booking directly through Discovery Parks moving forward!

Was a bit unsure when we booked this site as we usually go for much larger sites with much more in the way of activities but sure glad we booked here now! Had a beachfront cabin, short walk to the beach through the side access gate and loved watching the steam train go past on its way to Semaphore jetty.|The park was quiet enough for the children to play around the cabin, the playground and pools were very well maintained (albeit slightly cold in the pool) but the children loved spending a couple of hours there each afternoon/evening.|Loved the coffee truck, fairy floss and popcorn truck and the ice cream van that frequented the site.|Cabin was lovely and spacious. Did have to ask for some extra linen for the bunkbed as not made up and slightly more attention could have been paid when cleaning staff did the oven/hob. Also no baking tray in the cabin which I found quite odd. Wifi was pretty poor inside the cabin. Liked the extra touches of free tea, coffee, milk on arrival, much appreciated after an 8 hour car journey! |If the price was right we would definitely stay again
A good holiday park with some pet friendly cabins.
Stay here more than once for work luxury cabins good location and ocean views beautiful spot
Good a bit on the expensive side but clean amenities close to cbd
Great place to stay

Como todos estos camping todo hermoso y limpio. Lo mejor: la atención en recepción, gracias!

Nota: hay piscina, bbq y juegos pero no hay zona común solo para estar. Es importante para quienes estamos solo con carpa.
Great stuff guys

Stayed here over the weekend for my sons birthday and everything was great apart from the floors. Within the first 5 minutes of my son crawling around his legs and feet were completely black and I had to mop the floor witch turned the mop black as well. My sisters cabin floor was the same. I told the front office and they said they will tell management.
Great Caravan park. Awesome location.
厨房缺盆少玩的，微波炉是坏的，厕所把手坏了，下次不去了！

Nice and cosy
Sheets are lovely and clean
No Hand rails.
Great location beach bare feet and ocean Healing.
No where to wash the beach sand off.
lots of renovations and building going on. Good luck I hope your tenors work out.
We came on Sorry Business.
from the west
Boorda one
(see ya)

This is one of the best caravan 🚐 park(not star-rating motel or hotel) I'd been. Staff are friendly and helpful(except they upgraded me a double bed cabin for me n my 16yrs old boy instead of 2xsingle beds 🛌 that I booked for🤭🤭). Place is very facilitated and secured.

Spent almost $1000 for 2 weeks here on a powered site. Called up to see if we can check in a little earlier and nope appearantly check in is at 1pm! Bit of a joke since we paid a fair bit to be here..

It is a few minutes' walk from the beach. The beach is average, but we come from Western Australia and are very spoilt about stunning beaches. The sites are nice and spacious, and all amenities are clean and well-maintained. The staff is friendly and helpful. The city is about a 30-minute drive away.
Great park, so close to the beach…will definitely return in the not too distant future!!
Loved it
A great getaway for 10 people from Yorke Peninsula. Fortunate with the weather.. great location to explore Port Adelaide.

Great location with beach access, clean and tidy park and facilities with a large camp kitchen.
Has a pool and jumping pillow.
Friendly helpful team.
A little bit of road noise at night.

Don't be confused by there 'No smoking Policy', smoking is not allowed in communal areas, however is allowed on your site. When we arrived our designated Ensuite site was parallel with a smoking family. It was terrible! We asked to be relocated and they found us a vacant area- thank gosh! It took quite a lot of jumping up and down and causing a stink though.

We stayed here over the long weekend, it was clean and tidy, free upgrade upon check in which made us smile. Great views from the balcony of the beach and seeing the steam train. Children really enjoyed themselves on the playground. Everything was clean and tidy. They could do with fixing the cords on the blinds as they kept falling apart, that’s the only negative we had.

Lovely campground on the beachfront with easy access to the shore and walks. Pitches are not big, but facilities are excellent and very clean. The bbqs and camp kitchen offered lots of options for cooking and included toaster, kettle and microwave. There is Fort Glanville miniature railway station just outside the back gate and a 15.minute walk took us to the charming town of Semaphore with an excellent selection of cafes , eating places and a few shops. Overall an excellent campground
Ensuit site ckean tidy .Girls in office very polite and helpful.
Cabin was not very clean. Some of the sheets had stains. Staff didn't seem all that interested to be there and were not very concerned with our complaints.

Had an  absolutely amazing stay here! The staff were very friendly and extremely accommodating to my families needs and highly recommend staying here.
They are also pet friendly and very understanding when it comes to the fur babies. Nothing was too much trouble for any staff member. Also have to give them extra credit for putting up with my crazy partner lol
Best place to stay in Adelaide if you like to be near the beach.

Caravan park right on the beach
What more do you need
Nice folks at check in

I am updating my review. I honestly love this place so much. I just want to update on my most recent trip. For those needing a space that is mobility needs friendly I checked in and omg the cabin we stayed in. Amazing wide car space, flat entry and exit. Huge walk in/ wheel in if needed shower with a bench, rails next to the toilet and in the shower.  Extra power points. I was blown away.  See pics. I've always loved this park but more so now.

We have stayed here a few times and never had any issues. We have been here for 6 nights of an 8 night stay in preparation for the birth of my baby. My daughter and I were so comfortable and relaxed. The park is quiet and well maintained and secure. The cabin was lovely, beautiful and clean and with it having an oven and cook top and full size fridge we could cook and shop for the week. The beds were unbelievably comfortable. It's close to the beach and west lakes shopping centre. I will be sad to be leaving after such a lovely week but will be back.
Fiona, lauren and the staff run a spotless ship. I cant say enough how friendly and helpfull they are!!!

The Executive cabin we stayed in was way above our expectations. And 30% off for two nights Very modern from keyless entry to top quality fitout and contents. The kitchen cabinets were soft close and like a new home build! All contents were gleaming and clean. The bed head like a hotel room. The whole place was so clean, absolutely a pleasure. And Semaphore was a beautiful place to visit, we hadn’t heard of it before. Took sooo many photos of buildings and the jetty and foreshore. Two nights wasn’t quite enough.
Amazing place

Stayed with interstate family for a 4 day weekend. The staff were amazingly friendly. Stopping for a chat as they saw us. The cabins are neat and tidy. Just the way I remember from 26 years ago when I stayed for a National Titles, with a few modernisations added. It's great to see this place is dog friendly.

Great park next to dog friendly beach
This my second stay highly recommended

Stayed in one of the cabins and it was very very clean and comfortable.
Will be back again

Love this Caravan Park.  Parents were long term residents here.  Mum remains.  The amenities block is very good.  Can even have a bath!!
The walk to the beach is short and well worth it.  I have had many hours walking along the Semaphore beach with my mother.  So nice to have my mum so close to this lovely space.
A wonderful caravan Park and short stay respite place
Service was excellent with a smile 😊, stayed in my caravan ☺️,toilet & shower block absolutely clean as 👍👌👍, would recommend this place absolutely for shore 😉
Great spot, close to the city. Accomodation and surrounding property was incredibly well maintained. We enjoyed our stay, and will definitely be back!
Excellent beach through gate

Best park yet, flat ground, good ensuite although shower could be improved. Both from pressure and ensuring even after turning hot water tap on full and regulating, it still went cold.
Couldn't open windows of caravan as neighbour had radio going all night.
I didn't stay, I visited my friends in there caravan.👱‍♀️

Staying for 2 nights with a van and rang to ask about check in time and was informed your site is free and come in when ready, 11.15am! Never have we had a check in that earlier was a pleasant surprise 😊 good position to parks, beach and walking track. We ended staying 3 nights. Only a small thing and that is the matting on the sites need replacing as torn up but did see a lot of sites had concrete in place so maybe updating when they can! Thanks team
The place is 100% about the beachfront location. Gentle waves, easy walk to the town of Semaphore. Cute tourist train runs along the coast  on days when temps are <31°C.

Just the best location, caravan site are large, pool excellent, if you have kids great spot, beach just out the back gate, and amenities are clean and good water.
Yep, well done 👏 ✔️ just a great park.

Nice pool area right on the beach lovely pathway to walk beds comfortable definitely had fun
relaxing weekend 😀😌❤️
We stayed in a Pet friendly cabin and although it was dated it was perfectly clean, and had super comfy bed/pillows and crisp hotel linen. Great spot right by the beach, highly recommend.

Wirklich ein sehr schöner Campingplatz mit sehr vielen Möglichkeiten für Kinder :)
(Kartverlei, Pool, Trampolin, Sandspielplatz und keinem Spielplatz)

Absolute Empfehlung!

Great park stayed for 3 weeks enjoyed a quick 3 min walk to the beach great for any age excellent for small kids
2 pools in the park 1 for the littlest kids and a deep pool park is well maintained amenities are resonantly clean will stay again
Always clean and comfy. This place rocks.  And strolls on the beach are a 2 minute walk away  🤩
Fantastic Location

Stayed here many times and each time has been as expected. Clean, friendly service and great location. Close to beach and walking track with access through a gate. There is a minature train that runs past the discovery park that you can ride on. There is an onsite pool, playground, camp kitchen, bbq, games room. Plenty for the kids to do.

Great place to stay but if you have kids bicycles, recommend putting them in the room at night, had a trek marlin stolen Christmas Eve, they cut the locks, it was one of 3 that was stolen during our stay the other bikes was taken from other guests through out the park over 3 nights, was disappointed to wake up Christmas morning and see the bike missing, put a spoiler on Christmas morning for us including my young son who loved that bike so much, spoke to the management and they wasn't very keen to review footage, was told maybe later today
Enjoyed our stay.  Staff were great and helpful. Good location.
Messed up my falooda. Didn't fix it despite I highlight it. They have a "Take it or leave it" approach with 0 customer service.
Very bad WIFI CONNECTION AND THEY WON'T FIX IT NO MATTER HOW MANY TIMES YOU TELL THEM IT DOESNT WORK

Perfect location for a family stay with kids. All the amenities and entertainment to keep the kids occupied. Borders right on the beach with side gate access to the surf lifesaving club and the historical Fort Glanville.

Very tidy, clean an right on the beach. Only stayed one night. At 23:00 I went out to the car make sure the fridge freezer waz unplugged. As habit I closed the door behind me, and left the door card in the cabin. Rang the emergency number an spoke to a young man who come around in I swear waz 5mins or under. Thank you so much Sir. I didn't even ask your  name. That's truly the best service ever. So appreciated at that time of night. Yes I would certainly recommend camping up here. There got sites, for any style or mode of accommodation.  From swags, tents, self-contained, cabins and apartments. The best the bestist after hours assistance. Thanks again digger.

My partner and I stayed at the caravan park for 6 nights and was moderately pleased with our experience. We camped in a tent which isn’t the best for coastal conditions 😂
A few things to note about the caravan park:
- the camp kitchen was lovely. Unfortunately our car fridge stopped working so we had to use the camp kitchen facilities. The fridge however stunk because someone had left their yucky food to rot. Other than that it was a very clean facility. Other campers need to realise it’s a communal space and they should come and collect their dirty dishes and not leave ovens on unattended.
- The bathrooms were pretty clean but the only thing I was disappointed with was that every shower had a connecting drain/floor. I don’t really like the fact that when I’m showering and someone decides to use the shower right next to me their soap travels into the bottom of my shower and vice versa
- Our site was directly infront of the pool which I loved! I was swimming every day that I could and it was conveniently right there for me to use!
All in all it is a very nice spot to stay and very conveniently located for everything we wanted to do whilst we visited Adelaide and next time I will try and get an ensuite site instead :)

My overnight stay at Discovery Parks - Adelaide Beachfront was fantastic. The beachside campsite featured a massive inflatable play area on the sand, adding a fun touch. The pool was a refreshing bonus, and the spacious grassed site provided a comfortable camping experience. The camp kitchen was a standout, equipped with gas cookers and well-maintained amenities, including paper towels. Clean tables and seating added to the overall positive impression, making it a great spot for a short camping getaway.
Cabins were modern and well presented however very small. Good park amenities and beach access. Located right next to a large park as well.
Location is great , but comparing what you can pay for a holiday home rental to a top notch villa.. I will look elsewhere next time.
We rented a parking space and slept in our car.  The facilities are beautiful and super clean, the bathroom, showers, kitchen, barbecue area, everything very good

I frequent the park for work as I am a caravan motorhome and RV electrical and mechanical repairer, always friendly staff, always meticulously maintained and always greeted with a smile and friendly staff,

Great park, kids have quite a bit to do, washing machines x4 are $4 each ,amenities are good, a bit close together (parking with caravan), but that's what most parks are, ocean a 2 minute walk away. Would stay again.

I love this park. Not too huge & right on the beach. Older park but well kept & clean
Staff are friendly & so helpful, nothing ever seems too much trouble .
Lovely walk along the beach or Esplanade path to Semaphore jetty & Semaphore Road shops/cinemas

Stayed here for 2 nights with my family.
Pros:
-The standard cabin where we stayed was good size, modern and clean.
-very close to the beach that they have their own secured access.
-great location, heaps of restaurants and shops nearby
-2 good size pools
Cons:
-was only provided with 1 shampoo, conditioner and body wash for 3persons staying for 2 nights.
- TV was not a smart TV compared to most caravan parks we’ve been to which has smart tvs in modern cabins.
- playground and jumping pillow were quite small compared to the other Discovery Parks.
-Some of the vertical blind slats were missing that we had to cover the spaces with blanket every night for privacy
Nice comfortable cabin with good facilities. The park is pretty well laid out and the facilities seemed popular.
Good place to live, like a cosy house
Only stayed a night, van parks are very rare occurrence for us. Everything appeared clean and tidy. If we had to, we would stay again.

I just spent the last 4 nights here with my 2 dogs that were allowed in the pet friendly cabin. very et friendly site. backs onto the beach. great location. wonderful facilities and helpful, proactive staff. i would stay again. thanks for the wonderful stay.
Noisy.
it was our second time there. We only camped, no room taken. Highly Recommended. Clean, organised, beautiful location, but expensive for campsite

Caravan sites are good, especially if you opt for an ensuite. Great location, excellent staff. Maintenance staff are outstanding, constantly busy and doing a great job of keeping the place ship-shape.
A cabin in october and a ensuite powered camp site today.  Great facilities and location

Wow this place is great.
My 1st night of 3 here and I'm impressed.
Camp kitchen is incredible, quality gas burner stove, electric Hot plate stove, two flat plate BBQ surrounded by stainless benches and sink with great hot water. Is like a commercial kitchen setup...
Fridge and freezer spotless clean. Nice deck area to enjoy a meal.

Staff at check in are very cheerful, helpful and accommodating. Even with a fairly last minute walk in on a Saturday Arvo...

All amenities are super clean and spacious with multiple shower and toilet cubicles.

Very pet friendly which as far as I can see is the only park along coast north of Brighton that are...
All doggos so far have been friendly and minimal barking.. even my noisy thing...

Location is amazing.. shortcut through gate straight to beach..

Looking forward to my next couple of nights here and most likely many more

Very friendly staff makes our stays enjoyable, location is perfect. My husband has poor vision, I tow our caravan. Brian has assisted with my reverse parking which I appreciate. Your staff are a credit to your park

My husband and I stayed at this park for three days and highly recommend this park.  Incredibly clean,very pleasant staff and positioned well so that we could get out and about in
Adelaide and surrounding areas.   Great stay and very reasonable price.

Hi just wanted to say thank you all so much our grand kids had a ball driving the karts , bouncy pillow games room and all the other stuff . The staff were amazing so helpful and couldnt do enough for us. Us as the grand parents also loved the park and everything in it . I cant praise this park enough . Thank you all so much for everything we will be back the grannies can't wait . Cabins we're so very clean and so was the park it's self . Also you have a amazing Cleaners , maintenance crew we were watching them all driving around always busy . Well again thanks heaps see you all again too.  Also the pizza area on site was a great idea too .

Fantastic caravan park with very clean and great welcoming staff ✅

Recommend ✅🙌

We stayed in a cabin which was nice and clean,  the park is next to the beach and centrally located,  making getting around Adelaide quite easy.  Have already booked for return  with our caravan in the warmer weather.

Nice and quiet  close to the beach
Nice staff
